title: IdentityAssertionV1
description: Signed assertion from a trusted identity provider (OIDC issuer / SSO) vouching that the reviewer named in an approval_artifact.v1 is a real human with a specific role. Independent trust root from the approval issuer; both signatures must verify before /v1-evaluate issues a permit for an approval-required action. Wire-stable as `identity_assertion.v1`. The artifact carries the assertion under `identity_assertion`; the verifier rejects when `requireIdentityAssertion=true` and the field is absent.

principal_kind.description: Verifier requires 'human'. The other variants are present so the schema can describe denied / declined assertions structurally.

role.description: Required role attested by the IdP (e.g. 'qa_reviewer'). The verifier checks this equals the effective_required_role used to gate the action.

environment.description: Must equal expected_environment. Empty string allowed when the verifier was not given one (explicit equality, no implicit broadening).

kid.description: Key id within the issuer (for rotation). Looked up in IDENTITY_TRUSTED_ISSUERS.

nonce.description: Optional anti-replay nonce. The approval-artifact nonce is the primary defence; a deployment that wants assertion-level replay protection adds a separate ledger keyed on this field.

signature.description: Detached signature over the canonical assertion bytes with the `signature` field stripped. Algorithm is identified by issuer.kid in IDENTITY_TRUSTED_ISSUERS (HS256 hex or Ed25519 hex/base64url).
